reasoning: Initial observation suggests a linear relationship between A, B, and Y. Increasing A will help determine if Y changes in a predictable manner.
Since increasing A from 10.0 to 20.0 resulted in Y increasing from 15.0 to 25.0, it seems that Y is directly proportional to A. Now, I will check if B has any effect on Y by decreasing it.
Decreasing B from 5.0 to 0.0 caused Y to decrease from 25.0 to 20.0, suggesting that B also has a direct influence on Y. Given these observations, Y appears to be a linear combination of A and B.

From the observations, Y increases linearly with A and decreases with B. With A at its maximum value (30.0) and B at 0.0, Y is at its maximum value (30.0). This supports the hypothesis that Y = A - B.
